contextAuthentication.method: Exact raw byte count and standard SHA-256 for each declared file; each identity also matched exactly one source entry in the authenticated nested ledger.

summary: PASS. Independent review of the authenticated generic compiler found candidate construction, hard gates, scheduling, metrics, comparator order, canonical identities, reproducibility, and lifecycle claims deterministic, bounded, and accurately described. No verdict was inherited from Wave 1 or Audit B, and this specialist did not audit or approve Candidate A's own complete compilation.

artifacts.name: algorithm-lifecycle-review.md
artifacts.content:
# Algorithm And Lifecycle Review

Outcome: `PASS`

## Identity And Scope

This review executed only `review.algorithm-lifecycle` for goal `v11.specialist-compiler.release` revision 19, agent `agent.073738545314110329a947b77922c2a89c772cbaf4f2b9da29daa212e9aa9972`, blueprint `sha256:a4c0b3848a186b3723f8d6360345817c423d7bfd4e780b2efdd2a1552be97d54`, and Candidate A `team.e016556b8933ef6ead3b1d321bf187cee6a286ca7ca19332dcf6505fe1d02a7d`.

The review covers the generic compiler algorithm and its lifecycle claims. It does not audit Candidate A's own complete compilation, selected roster, rendered package, or cross-package authorization. Those exact-compilation duties remain with Audit B and the external host.

## Authentication

- The specialist contract matched 13,789 raw bytes and `sha256:e2a6d117973af85a9d65b249e1aab5886beca0a3169f6e81fca3ccc3a7ea2dbf`.
- An independent domain-separated recomputation over the embedded blueprint, excluding `contentDigest`, reproduced `sha256:a4c0b3848a186b3723f8d6360345817c423d7bfd4e780b2efdd2a1552be97d54`.
- The dependency handoff matched 32,202 raw bytes and `sha256:f12b044d9398bc270d7e8d79e52d077296df3aecd7dd251d532a77aadd10b045`. Its exact identities bind `prepare.candidate`, agent `agent.a9685b45ac9e2935d8c1eba48731524171f963ab6e380ec93fd9a2b691387730`, Candidate A's supplied compilation/package pair, and outcome `pass`.
- The inline `review-candidate-digests.json` ledger matched 24,357 UTF-8 bytes and `sha256:47920dc4ea5002a7c82e21d04fcab94e64a1501f25ca25d9d02af17c5ed024f2`. It reports 35 identity-and-content bindings and explicitly reports semantic review as not performed.
- All 15 context items declared to this specialist independently matched their contract byte counts and SHA-256 digests, and each matched its unique entry in the authenticated 35-source ledger. No source was read for semantic review before this authentication completed.
- Candidate compilation `sha256:a2c43d26932e169707129065da52bdbef0b6e525a95cd0adf30d7a22c71ae807` and package `sha256:d273142d00b9b960c99afb61d0e4236dec94b48d074a579dbef1ce8d486a94ed` are treated as externally host-verified identity facts only. No semantic verdict was inherited from Wave 1, Audit B, or the dependency binder.

## Candidate Construction

`normalizeGoal` and `normalizePartition` sort all selection-relevant arrays and group members before construction (`src/specialist-compiler.ts:396`, `:855`). Candidate records are keyed by canonical partition JSON, so duplicate structural and proposed routes merge without influencing ranking through labels or insertion order (`:940`, `:952`).

For one through eight work units, `enumeratePartitions` generates the restricted-growth set partitions up to `min(maxAgents, workUnitCount)` (`:975`, `:1080`). The authenticated tests assert Bell counts `1, 2, 5, 15, 52, 203, 877, 4140` and the five-unit/two-agent capped count `16` (`test/specialist-compiler.test.mjs:587`). A separate read-only contract-model oracle independently reproduced those counts.

For nine through 64 work units, `buildCandidates` constructs only the documented bounded set: serial, weight-balanced per permitted group count, Module, dependency-level, evidence-duty, scope, and supplied proposal partitions. Structural candidates over `maxAgents` are omitted and canonical duplicates merge. The emitted claim is exactly `bounded_evaluated_set_no_global_optimum`; neither implementation output fields nor the normative contract claim a global optimum (`src/specialist-compiler.ts:1080`, `:1921`; `test/specialist-compiler.test.mjs:734`).

## Hard Gates

Goal-level validation runs before candidate construction and closes blocking decisions, references, DAG shape, Module consistency, evidence ownership, context allowlists, repository scope coverage, capabilities, permissions, and authority ceilings (`src/specialist-compiler.ts:469`, `:837`, `:871`).

Each candidate then rejects agent-ceiling violations, missing, duplicate, or unknown work units; derived agent dependency cycles; and producer/checker co-location when independence is requested (`:1210`, `:1480`, `:1521`). The producer/checker gate compares every independent verifier or reviewer owner against every producer owner for the same criterion. The authenticated tests cover goal cycles, partition-induced cycles, authority/context/evidence failures, blocking decisions, and the under-split independence case (`test/specialist-compiler.test.mjs:921`, `:1017`, `:1104`).

No path was found that lets an ineligible candidate enter `compareEligibleCandidates` or become selected. If no eligible candidate exists, `compileRequest` returns `SC4306` with no roster.

## Scheduling And Metrics

The fixed scheduler derives cross-agent dependencies after grouping, topologically orders agent IDs, computes remaining critical-path lengths, and chooses ready work by longer remaining critical path, longer duration, then agent ID (`src/specialist-compiler.ts:1167`, `:1282`, `:1303`). It enforces `maxConcurrency`, serializes exact write/write, write/read, read/write, and shared conflict-zone intersections, permits read/read overlap, and delays successors by `handoffCost`.

Metric construction at `:1399` matches the normative meanings: makespan is maximum finish; peak concurrency is interval overlap; conflict pairs use exact intersections; handoffs are distinct cross-agent dependency edges; duplicated context bytes and permission tokens count cross-agent repetition; and total work, startup, and handoff costs use the declared integer model.

The authenticated scheduler tests cover critical-path priority, duration and agent-ID ties, concurrency, conflict serialization, and handoff release (`test/specialist-compiler.test.mjs:611` through `:713`). The independent oracle recomputed all expected metrics and selected partitions for the six authenticated golden fixtures: one-agent-optimal, genuinely-parallel, under-split, over-split, conflict-heavy, and generic-role.

## Comparator And Selection

`compareEligibleCandidates` implements the required lexicographic order without extra fields: projected makespan, conflict pairs, handoff count, duplicated context bytes, duplicated permission scopes, agent count, then canonical partition JSON (`src/specialist-compiler.ts:1580`). `selectionReason` compares the winner to the mandatory serial baseline using that same field order and reports serial rejection codes when the baseline is ineligible (`:1619`, `:1628`).

Proposal IDs, proposal order, origins, evaluation digests, and candidate generation order are not comparator inputs. The selected candidate is the first eligible value after the deterministic comparator; alternatives and proposal evaluations are presentation/evidence outputs only.

## Reproducibility And Digests

Input is snapshotted into detached, deeply frozen JSON while rejecting proxies, accessors, cycles, unsupported prototypes, invalid numbers, excessive depth, and excessive nodes (`src/snapshot.ts:56`, `:178`). Goal, proposal, partition, permission, Module-port, context-use, evidence, scope, dependency, handoff, and stop-condition arrays are normalized before compilation.

Canonical JSON sorts object keys and rejects lone surrogates. Domain-separated SHA-256 frames include a fixed prefix plus length-prefixed domain and payload bytes (`src/canonical-json.ts:194`, `:225`, `:238`). Goal, authority, partition, agent-group, candidate-evaluation, evaluation-set, blueprint, and compilation identities use distinct domains. Candidate and agent IDs retain the full 64-hex SHA-256 suffix. `evaluationSetDigest` binds candidate IDs and evaluation digests in candidate-ID order; the compilation digest binds the normalized complete result (`src/specialist-compiler.ts:1868`).

The authenticated permutation test reverses object keys and all logical input arrays, proposal groups, and group members, then requires deep, canonical, evaluation-set, and compilation-digest equality (`test/specialist-compiler.test.mjs:834`). Direct source tracing found no remaining selection input that bypasses normalization or a deterministic tie-break. The stop condition for permutation-sensitive selection was not triggered.

## Lifecycle Boundary

The reviewed compiler sources construct and return values. `src/specialist-compiler.ts` has no filesystem, network, process, provider, launch, merge, or memory-effect import; `src/canonical-json.ts` uses only `node:crypto` for hashing and `src/snapshot.ts` uses only `node:util` for proxy detection. The normative contract limits schema loading to immutable packaged resources and assigns materialization, context delivery, permission enforcement, actual scheduling, execution, persistence, integration, merge, and memory updates to an external host.

`launchWaves` are explicitly projected start groups, not dispatch receipts or capacity reservations. The two-phase protocol correctly prevents this specialist from claiming review of Candidate A's own complete compilation and requires a separate frozen Audit B package for that duty.

## Executed Checks

- Raw byte-count and SHA-256 authentication passed for the contract, dependency handoff, nested ledger, and all 15 declared context files.
- Blueprint digest recomputation passed under `swecircuit.specialist.blueprint.v1`.
- Dependency and nested-ledger structural identity checks passed; the ledger remained identity-only with no semantic claim.
- Source-to-contract review passed for construction, bounded/exact claims, gates, scheduler, metrics, comparator, digests, and lifecycle boundaries.
- A read-only independent Node oracle recomputed the six golden fixture outcomes, exact Bell counts through eight units, and the capped two-group count; all assertions passed.
- The repository test suite was not executed because the authenticated test file imports undeclared `dist` and transitive build inputs outside this specialist's read scope. No unbound build output was used as evidence.

## Findings And Residual Risk

No algorithm or lifecycle finding requiring `REVISE` was identified. Bounded search is honestly non-global, and no logical-input permutation path was found that changes selection.

Residual model risks remain as documented: owner-supplied weights and exact conflict keys can be wrong; bounded search can miss an unconstructed better partition; and projected waves do not predict runtime capacity or enforce isolation. Any mutation to a bound source, contract, candidate identity, or package identity invalidates this review and requires reauthentication and a new specialist revision.

## Conclusion

The authenticated generic compiler implementation and its declared evidence support deterministic, bounded, and accurately described candidate construction, scheduling, selection, digest semantics, and lifecycle boundaries for `criterion.algorithm`. Outcome: `PASS`.

evidence.statement: Authenticated source tracing plus an independent read-only contract-model oracle support deterministic and accurately bounded construction, scheduling, selection, digest, reproducibility, and lifecycle semantics.

checks.method: Exact raw byte count and standard SHA-256.

commands.command: PowerShell Get-Item, Get-FileHash -Algorithm SHA256, and Get-Content -Raw on the exact specialist contract.
PowerShell SHA256.Create/ComputeHash and ConvertFrom-Json over the exact dependency handoff and its inline ledger.
PowerShell Get-Item/Get-FileHash loop over the 15 declared context paths with unique nested-ledger matching.
rg and Get-Content over only the authenticated compiler, canonicalization, snapshot, types, contract, spec, two-phase protocol, tests, and fixture contexts.
node -e with a base64-transported read-only script to parse the authenticated contract and recompute the domain-separated blueprint digest.
node -e with a base64-transported independent contract model over the six authenticated fixtures and exact partition counts.
PowerShell fail-closed structural assertions for dependency goal, agent, blueprint, candidate, work unit, ledger identity/scope, and no-semantic-claim fields.
node -e SHA-256 and UTF-8 byte-count calculation over the in-memory algorithm-lifecycle-review.md content.

assumptions: The external-host compilation and package launch facts are trusted only as already verified identity bindings; this review does not reproduce or semantically approve Candidate A's exact compilation or package.

The authenticated test file is reviewed as executable evidence design, not treated as a test-run result; implementation test execution would require undeclared dist and transitive build inputs outside this specialist's read scope.
Owner-reviewed work-unit weights, context declarations, exact scope keys, and conflict zones remain input-model assumptions rather than facts the compiler can prove.
risks: Bounded search can miss a better unconstructed partition, although the emitted claim states this limitation exactly.
Projected makespan and launch waves depend on declared relative weights and exact conflict keys and are not runtime promises, capacity reservations, or isolation evidence.
No repository test suite was executed in this lane because doing so would read undeclared build output; confidence comes from authenticated source review and the independent fixture oracle.
Any mutation to a bound source or identity retires this evidence and requires complete reauthentication and review.
failedAttempts: The first nested-ledger hash command used the unavailable static SHA256.HashData API and failed before extraction; the read-only retry used SHA256.Create/ComputeHash and passed.

Four inline Node transport attempts failed before blueprint parsing because of PowerShell backtick/native-quote handling and an unavailable btoa helper; a base64-transported read-only Node command then recomputed the blueprint digest successfully. None of these attempts wrote files or changed review state.
followUps: The integration owner must validate and preserve this exact raw handoff and its inline artifact, then combine it only with the other independent review lanes bound to the same Candidate A digest pair.
A dedicated release-verification lane with the required broader context should execute and preserve the canonical full gate; this algorithm reviewer did not widen authority to do so.
Any change to the goal, contract, declared source bytes, compilation digest, package digest, or dependency handoff requires a new candidate revision and a fresh algorithm review.
